payload.query: How has increased factionalism among Republicans in Congress affected partisan polarization in the institution? What implications has this had for partisan theories of legislative organization, namely Conditional Party Government and Cartel Theory?

articles.title: A Diminished Congress Weighs Whether to Reassert Its Power
articles.abstract: Lawmakers head into President Trump’s second year facing questions about whether they can reclaim congressional clout in the face of his power grab.
articles.snippet: Lawmakers head into President Trump’s second year facing questions about whether they can reclaim congressional clout in the face of his power grab.

articles.title: Indiana Lawmakers Reject Trump’s New Political Map
articles.abstract: Republicans hold an overwhelming majority in the Indiana Senate, but more than a dozen of them defied the president’s wishes, voting against a map aimed at adding Republicans in Congress.
articles.snippet: Republicans hold an overwhelming majority in the Indiana Senate, but more than a dozen of them defied the president’s wishes, voting against a map aimed at adding Republicans in Congress.

articles.title: Senate Advances Measure to Curb Trump’s Use of Force in Venezuela
articles.abstract: A handful of Republicans joined Democrats to allow the measure to move forward, reflecting concerns in both parties about the administration’s strategy.
articles.snippet: A handful of Republicans joined Democrats to allow the measure to move forward, reflecting concerns in both parties about the administration’s strategy.
articles.byline: By Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
keywords: United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Democrats See No Need to Capitulate, Nor Republicans to Cut a Deal
articles.abstract: The last time Senate Democrats found themselves taking the blame for a government shutdown, they quickly caved. That’s less likely to happen now.
articles.snippet: The last time Senate Democrats found themselves taking the blame for a government shutdown, they quickly caved. That’s less likely to happen now.
keywords: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (2010)

articles.title: Trump Presses on With Tariffs Despite Court, and Court of Public Opinion
articles.abstract: The president is plowing ahead with an unpopular policy after the Supreme Court’s rebuke, deepening his party’s risk in the midterm elections.
articles.snippet: The president is plowing ahead with an unpopular policy after the Supreme Court’s rebuke, deepening his party’s risk in the midterm elections.
articles.byline: By Shane Goldmacher and Katie Glueck

articles.title: Trump Shows His Power, and Greene Reveals His Weakness
articles.abstract: As the president forced a onetime loyalist from Congress, her defiant departure signaled a coming debate over Republican identity in a post-Trump era.
articles.snippet: As the president forced a onetime loyalist from Congress, her defiant departure signaled a coming debate over Republican identity in a post-Trump era.
articles.byline: By Lisa Lerer and Reid J. Epstein

articles.title: Democrats Reach Spending Deal With Trump, Seeking to Rein In ICE
articles.abstract: Democrats and the White House agreed to fund the Department of Homeland Security for two weeks while they negotiate restrictions on an immigration crackdown. Senators said they hoped to vote on the deal on Friday.
articles.snippet: Democrats and the White House agreed to fund the Department of Homeland Security for two weeks while they negotiate restrictions on an immigration crackdown. Senators said they hoped to vote on the deal on Friday.
articles.byline: By Catie Edmondson and Carl Hulse

articles.title: Republicans Undercut Johnson, Circumventing Him to Force Votes
articles.abstract: The speaker has repeatedly lost his grip on the House floor thanks to a once rare parliamentary maneuver that G.O.P. members are increasingly using to force action on legislation.
articles.snippet: The speaker has repeatedly lost his grip on the House floor thanks to a once rare parliamentary maneuver that G.O.P. members are increasingly using to force action on legislation.

articles.title: The Battle for Congress Is Kicking Off. Here’s What to Know.
articles.abstract: Primary contests in Texas could help decide control of the House and Senate in November, and North Carolina and Arkansas are also holding elections on Tuesday.
articles.snippet: Primary contests in Texas could help decide control of the House and Senate in November, and North Carolina and Arkansas are also holding elections on Tuesday.

articles.title: Cornyn, Paxton, Hunt: It’s a Republican Showdown in Texas
articles.abstract: Times Opinion convened a panel of state experts to weigh in on the Senate Republican primary.
